name: Eggplant in the Style of Modugno (Melanzane A
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Freshly grated Parmesan cheese (2 oz.)
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the eggplant slices in a colander and sprinkle with salt. Set aside to drain for at least 1-1/2 hours, gently squeezing out excess liquid, then pat dry with paper towels.
2. Heat olive oil in a large skillet over moderately high heat until it reaches 375°F or a small bread cube browns in about 1 minute. Fry the eggplant slices in batches, turning once, until golden and cooked through, about 2 minutes per side. Remove with a slotted spoon and drain on paper towels.
3. Preheat the oven to 375°F. In a medium bowl, combine bread crumbs, garlic, parsley, mozzarella, and all but 2 tablespoons of Parmesan. Stir in egg and season with salt and pepper.
4. Spoon 3/4 cup Marinara Sauce into a nonreactive 8-by-12-inch baking pan. Spread about 1 tablespoon of the mozzarella mixture on each slice of eggplant, roll up seam side down, and place in the pan. Spoon remaining 1/4 cup Marinara Sauce over rolls and sprinkle reserved 2 tablespoons Parmesan on top. (This dish can be prepared to this point several hours ahead and kept covered at room temperature.)
5. Bake until mixture bubbles and cheese is melted, about 20 minutes. Serve hot or at room temperature. These eggplant rolls are from Apulia, the region that forms the heel of the Italian boot. They make a wonderful antipasto or delicious luncheon dish.
